message:
fix: normalize robot output envelope across all commands (bd-x1tm)

Standardize the four envelope fields (generated_at, data_hash,
output_format, version) across all robot commands. Previously,
several commands used time.Time instead of RFC3339 strings,
lacked data_hash, or had no envelope at all.

Changes:
- Embed RobotEnvelope in inline output structs (hotspots, file-beads,
  impact, relations, blocker-chain, sprint-list, forecast, capacity,
  burndown)
- Wrap external types (orphans, impact-network, causality,
  related-work) with output_format and version fields
- Sprint-show now wraps model.Sprint under "sprint" key with full
  envelope instead of raw output
- Update schema definition to include output_format and version
- Add TestRobotEnvelopeConsistency e2e test
- Update TestRobotSprintShow_Found for new envelope structure

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"bd-37ki","title":"Add fallback release notes workflow for manual releases","description":"When GoReleaser fails and binaries are uploaded manually through GitHub UI, the release gets no release notes. Need a fallback workflow that auto-generates notes for any release with minimal/empty body.

Implementation:

1. Create .github/workflows/release-notes-fallback.yml:
   triggers:
     on:
       release:
         types: [created, edited]
   permissions:
     contents: write

2. Detection logic:
   - Fetch release body via gh api
   - Skip if body length >= 100 chars (already has meaningful notes)
   - Skip if body contains "## " (has markdown headers = intentional content)
   - Skip if body contains idempotency marker "<!-- auto-generated-notes -->"
   - Skip draft releases (only process published releases)

3. Previous tag detection:
   ```bash
   CURRENT_TAG="${{ github.event.release.tag_name }}"
   PREVIOUS_TAG=$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0 --exclude="$CURRENT_TAG" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
   if [ -z "$PREVIOUS_TAG" ]; then
     PREVIOUS_TAG=$(git rev-list --max-parents=0 HEAD | head -1)
   fi
   ```

4. Generate release notes via GitHub API:
   gh api repos/$REPO/releases/generate-notes \
     -f tag_name="$CURRENT_TAG" \
     -f previous_tag_name="$PREVIOUS_TAG" \
     --jq .body

5. Apply changelog filters matching .goreleaser.yaml (lines 33-38):
   - Exclude lines starting with "* docs:" or "* test:" or "* chore:"
   - Preserve all other commit types (feat, fix, refactor, perf, etc.)

6. Append idempotency marker to prevent duplicate runs:
   BODY="$GENERATED_NOTES\n\n<!-- auto-generated-notes -->"

7. Update release:
   gh api repos/$REPO/releases/$RELEASE_ID \
     -X PATCH \
     -f body="$BODY"

8. Error handling:
   - GitHub API rate limit (429): retry with exponential backoff (3 attempts)
   - Permission errors (403): fail with clear message about token permissions
   - Empty generated notes: append "No notable changes in this release." instead of leaving blank
   - Tag format validation: support both v-prefixed (v1.2.3) and bare (1.2.3) tags
   - First release (no previous tag): include all commits from initial commit

EDGE CASES:
- Release body has markdown but < 100 chars: check for "## " header to avoid overwriting intentional content
- Previous tag is ancient (1000+ commits): GitHub API handles pagination, notes may be truncated
- Multiple releases created rapidly: each triggers independently; idempotency marker prevents double-write
- GoReleaser runs AFTER manual release: GoReleaser updates body, next edit event re-triggers but marker prevents overwrite
- Non-semver tag format (release-20260206): git describe still works, API generates notes normally
- Release body has HTML entities: API returns raw markdown, no entity issues

TESTS:

Integration Tests (GitHub Actions workflow_dispatch for manual testing):
- test_manual_release_empty_body: create release with empty body -> notes generated
- test_manual_release_short_body: body = "bugfix" -> notes generated (< 100 chars)
- test_manual_release_long_body: body = 200 chars of real notes -> skipped (has content)
- test_manual_release_with_headers: body = "## Notes\nSomething" -> skipped (has ## header)
- test_idempotency: run workflow twice -> body unchanged on second run (marker detected)
- test_first_release: no previous tag -> all commits included
- test_draft_release: draft release -> skipped (only published)

E2E Tests (run in CI after workflow creation):
- test_goreleaser_then_fallback: GoReleaser succeeds -> fallback skips (body already populated)
- test_goreleaser_fail_then_manual: GoReleaser fails, manual upload -> fallback fills notes
- test_filter_docs_test_commits: verify docs: and test: commits excluded from generated notes
- test_empty_changelog: only docs/test commits between tags -> "No notable changes" message

Manual Verification Checklist:
- [ ] Create manual release via GitHub UI with empty body
- [ ] Verify workflow triggers within 60 seconds
- [ ] Verify release body populated with filtered changelog
- [ ] Verify idempotency marker present in body source
- [ ] Re-trigger workflow -> body unchanged
- [ ] Compare output with GoReleaser-generated notes for consistency

- test_empty_changelog: only docs/test commits between tags -> "No notable changes" message

Manual Verification Checklist:

 {"id":"bd-3fqy","title":"TOON: Update --help and robot help docs","description":"Document TOON format in CLI help.

## Files
- `cmd/bv/main.go` - Flag help text
- README.md - Usage examples

## Help Text
```
OUTPUT FORMAT
  --format toon    Token-optimized output (~50% fewer tokens than JSON)
  BV_OUTPUT_FORMAT=toon   Environment variable alternative
```

## README Addition
```markdown
### Token-Optimized Output (TOON)

For AI agents, TOON format reduces token usage by ~50%:

\`\`\`bash
bv --robot-triage --format toon
bv --robot-insights --format toon
export BV_OUTPUT_FORMAT=toon
\`\`\`
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T07:20:37.084755450Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T07:06:50.264355110Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T07:06:50.264332086Z","close_reason":"Already implemented - --robot-help includes format documentation, --format flag has help text","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-sfad","title":"Test: release notes fallback workflow correctness","description":"## What
Test the release-notes-fallback workflow (bd-t8dx) to verify it correctly populates empty releases.

## Test Plan

### Test 1: Workflow YAML validation
1. Run `gh workflow list -R Dicklesworthstone/beads_viewer` to verify workflow is registered
2. Validate YAML syntax: `python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open(\".github/workflows/release-notes-fallback.yml\"))"` 
3. Verify trigger is `on: release: types: [created, edited]`
4. Log: workflow name, triggers, jobs

### Test 2: Idempotency marker test
1. Create a draft release with body containing "<!-- auto-generated -->"
2. Trigger workflow (or simulate the logic)
3. Verify: body is NOT overwritten (idempotency marker detected)
4. Delete draft release
5. Log: release body before/after

### Test 3: Empty release gets notes
1. Create a draft release with empty body
2. Run the generate-notes API: `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/releases/generate-notes -f tag_name=v0.14.4`
3. Verify: non-empty notes returned
4. Delete draft release
5. Log: generated notes content, character count

### Test 4: Existing notes are preserved
1. Create a draft release with body "Manual release notes here"
2. Verify: workflow would NOT overwrite (body length > 50 chars)
3. Delete draft release
4. Log: body content, skip reason

## Acceptance Criteria
- YAML is valid and workflow registers
- Idempotency prevents double-write
- Empty releases get auto-generated notes
- Manual releases are not overwritten","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:37:07.112634091Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:37:11.747019517Z","external_ref":"gh:Dicklesworthstone/beads_viewer#99/test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","release","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-sfad","depends_on_id":"bd-t8dx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:37:11.746934358Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-t8dx","title":"Add release notes fallback workflow for manual releases (GH #99)","description":"## Context
GitHub issue #99 filed by qiviut (2026-02-06). Releases created by GoReleaser (via GH Actions) have full changelogs, but manually-created releases have no release notes. Example: v0.14.3 (GoReleaser) has notes, v0.14.4 (manual) has none.

## Owner Response
Confirmed the gap. When GoReleaser fails, binaries are uploaded manually via GitHub UI with minimal description. Proposed adding a fallback workflow triggered on release: [created] events.

## Current State
- Only workflow: .github/workflows/release.yml (triggers on push: tags: ['v*'], runs GoReleaser)
- GoReleaser config: .goreleaser.yaml (changelog: sort: asc, filters: exclude: ['^docs:', '^test:'])
- Beads bd-3v9s and bd-37ki created to track this, but NO actual workflow file was added
- Two commits (e0a1bd8, b50820a) only added beads entries, not implementation

## Implementation Plan
Create .github/workflows/release-notes-fallback.yml that:
1. Triggers on release: types: [created, edited]
2. Checks if release body is empty or minimal (< 50 characters)
3. Uses GitHub API: gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/releases/generate-notes -f tag_name=$TAG
4. Updates release body: gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/releases/{id} -X PATCH -f body="$NOTES"
5. Includes idempotency marker (<!-- auto-generated -->) so it doesn't overwrite manually written notes on subsequent edits

## Key Files
- NEW: .github/workflows/release-notes-fallback.yml
- EXISTING: .github/workflows/release.yml (reference for trigger patterns)
- EXISTING: .goreleaser.yaml (reference for changelog config)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:17:19.887335054Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T17:46:50.221771136Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T17:46:50.221749706Z","close_reason":"Enhanced release-notes.yml with edited trigger, 50-char threshold, idempotency marker, previous tag detection, and API-based note generation with fallback.","external_ref":"gh:Dicklesworthstone/beads_viewer#99","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","release","workflow"],"comments":[{"id":67,"issue_id":"bd-t8dx","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation: Create .github/workflows/release-notes-fallback.yml triggered on release created/edited. Step 1: check if body has idempotency marker or >50 chars (skip if so). Step 2: generate notes via gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/releases/generate-notes, append marker, PATCH release. Key: idempotency via HTML comment, 50-char threshold preserves manual notes, previous tag detection for accurate diff. See bd-sfad for test plan.","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:39:10Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-xgf7","title":"TOON: Create tru binary wrapper (pkg/robot/toon.go)","description":"Create Go wrapper for toon_rust tru binary.

## File
`pkg/robot/toon.go`

## Implementation
Use shell-out pattern from /data/projects/templates/toon_go_template.go:

```go
func encodeToon(payload any) (string, error) {
    jsonBytes, err := json.Marshal(payload)
    if err != nil {
        return "", err
    }
    trPath, err := getToonBinary()
    if err != nil {
        return "", err
    }
    cmd := exec.Command(trPath)
    cmd.Stdin = bytes.NewReader(jsonBytes)
    // ...
}
```

## Dependencies
- toon_rust tru binary must be installed
- TOON_TRU_BIN env var for custom path","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T07:16:31.960405372Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T07:06:17.263276562Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T07:06:17.263256955Z","close_reason":"Already implemented in cmd/bv/main.go using toon-go package - toonRobotEncoder struct handles encoding","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bv-03h1","title":"Worker Health Monitoring and Watchdog Recovery","description":"## PURPOSE
Detect when the BackgroundWorker goroutine has died or become unresponsive,
and automatically recover. The UI should never become permanently stale due
to a silent worker failure.

## PROBLEM
Goroutines can fail silently:
- Panic not caught by recover()
- Deadlock on channel operation
- Infinite loop in processing
- Runtime crash in C code (rare but possible)

If the worker dies:
- File changes are no longer detected
- UI shows increasingly stale data
- User has no indication anything is wrong
- Only fix is to restart bv

## RATIONALE
Production systems need watchdogs. The UI depends on the worker goroutine
being alive. We must detect failure and recover automatically.

## SOLUTION

### 1. Heartbeat Mechanism

```go
type BackgroundWorker struct {
    // ... existing fields ...
    
    heartbeatCh chan struct{}     // Worker sends heartbeat
    lastHeartbeat time.Time       // Last received heartbeat
    watchdogCtx   context.Context
    watchdogCancel context.CancelFunc
}

// Worker sends heartbeat periodically
func (w *BackgroundWorker) run() {
    defer w.wg.Done()
    
    heartbeatTicker := time.NewTicker(5 * time.Second)
    defer heartbeatTicker.Stop()
    
    for {
        select {
        case <-w.ctx.Done():
            return
            
        case <-w.watcher.Changed():
            w.handleFileChange()
            
        case <-heartbeatTicker.C:
            // Send heartbeat
            select {
            case w.heartbeatCh <- struct{}{}:
            default:
                // Channel full, that's fine
            }
        }
    }
}
```

### 2. Watchdog Goroutine

```go
// startWatchdog monitors worker health and restarts if needed
func (w *BackgroundWorker) startWatchdog() {
    w.watchdogCtx, w.watchdogCancel = context.WithCancel(context.Background())
    
    go func() {
        ticker := time.NewTicker(10 * time.Second)
        defer ticker.Stop()
        
        for {
            select {
            case <-w.watchdogCtx.Done():
                return
                
            case <-w.heartbeatCh:
                w.lastHeartbeat = time.Now()
                
            case <-ticker.C:
                w.checkHealth()
            }
        }
    }()
}

func (w *BackgroundWorker) checkHealth() {
    if time.Since(w.lastHeartbeat) > 30*time.Second {
        log.Printf("Worker heartbeat missed for 30s, attempting recovery")
        w.attemptRecovery()
    }
}
```

### 3. Recovery Logic

```go
func (w *BackgroundWorker) attemptRecovery() {
    // Increment recovery counter
    w.recoveryCount++
    if w.recoveryCount > 3 {
        log.Printf("Worker failed to recover after 3 attempts")
        // Send error to UI
        select {
        case w.errorCh <- SnapshotErrorMsg{
            Err:         errors.New("background worker unresponsive"),
            Recoverable: false,
        }:
        default:
        }
        return
    }
    
    // Cancel old context
    w.cancel()
    
    // Wait briefly for cleanup
    done := make(chan struct{})
    go func() {
        w.wg.Wait()
        close(done)
    }()
    
    select {
    case <-done:
        // Clean exit
    case <-time.After(5 * time.Second):
        log.Printf("Worker didn't exit cleanly, forcing restart")
    }
    
    // Restart
    w.ctx, w.cancel = context.WithCancel(context.Background())
    w.wg.Add(1)
    go w.run()
    
    // Trigger refresh
    w.triggerProcessing()
    
    log.Printf("Worker recovered (attempt %d)", w.recoveryCount)
}
```

### 4. Health Status Exposure

```go
type WorkerHealth struct {
    Alive         bool
    LastHeartbeat time.Time
    RecoveryCount int
    UptimeSince   time.Time
}

func (w *BackgroundWorker) Health() WorkerHealth {
    return WorkerHealth{
        Alive:         w.started.Load() && time.Since(w.lastHeartbeat) < 30*time.Second,
        LastHeartbeat: w.lastHeartbeat,
        RecoveryCount: w.recoveryCount,
        UptimeSince:   w.startTime,
    }
}
```

### 5. UI Health Indicator

```go
func (m Model) renderHealthIndicator() string {
    if m.backgroundWorker == nil {
        return ""
    }
    
    health := m.backgroundWorker.Health()
    if !health.Alive {
        return lipgloss.NewStyle().
            Foreground(lipgloss.Color("196")).
            Render("⚠ Worker unresponsive")
    }
    
    if health.RecoveryCount > 0 {
        return lipgloss.NewStyle().
            Foreground(lipgloss.Color("214")).
            Render(fmt.Sprintf("↻ Recovered x%d", health.RecoveryCount))
    }
    
    return ""
}
```

### 6. Panic Recovery in Worker

```go
func (w *BackgroundWorker) run() {
    defer w.wg.Done()
    defer func() {
        if r := recover(); r != nil {
            log.Printf("Worker panic recovered: %v\n%s", r, debug.Stack())
            // Watchdog will detect missed heartbeats and restart
        }
    }()
    
    // ... main loop
}
```

## TESTING

```go
func TestWatchdog_DetectsDeadWorker(t *testing.T) {
    worker := createTestWorker(t)
    worker.Start()
    
    // Simulate worker death by stopping heartbeats
    worker.stopHeartbeats()
    
    // Wait for watchdog to detect
    time.Sleep(35 * time.Second)
    
    // Should have attempted recovery
    require.Greater(t, worker.recoveryCount, 0)
}

func TestWatchdog_RecoversPanickedWorker(t *testing.T) {
    worker := createTestWorker(t)
    worker.Start()
    
    // Get initial snapshot
    <-worker.snapshotCh
    
    // Inject panic on next processing
    worker.injectPanic = true
    worker.TriggerRefresh()
    
    // Wait for recovery
    time.Sleep(35 * time.Second)
    
    // Should be alive again
    health := worker.Health()
    require.True(t, health.Alive)
    require.Equal(t, 1, health.RecoveryCount)
}

func TestWatchdog_GivesUpAfterMaxRetries(t *testing.T) {
    worker := createTestWorkerWithBrokenRun(t)
    worker.Start()
    
    // Wait for all recovery attempts
    time.Sleep(2 * time.Minute)
    
    // Should have given up
    require.Equal(t, 3, worker.recoveryCount)
    
    // Should have sent unrecoverable error
    err := <-worker.errorCh
    require.False(t, err.Recoverable)
}

func TestWatchdog_HeartbeatResetAfterRecovery(t *testing.T) {
    worker := createTestWorker(t)
    worker.Start()
    
    initialHeartbeat := worker.lastHeartbeat
    time.Sleep(6 * time.Second)
    
    // Heartbeat should have updated
    require.True(t, worker.lastHeartbeat.After(initialHeartbeat))
}
```

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Worker sends heartbeat every 5 seconds
- [ ] Watchdog checks health every 10 seconds
- [ ] Recovery triggered after 30 seconds no heartbeat
- [ ] Max 3 recovery attempts before giving up
- [ ] Panic in worker caught and recovered
- [ ] Health status exposed to UI
- [ ] UI shows indicator when worker unhealthy
- [ ] Tests verify detection and recovery
